pgbackrest.name: repoN-type
pgbackrest.description: Type of storage used for the repository. Operators set this automatically from repos[] union member (volume->posix on repo host, s3, gcs, azure). sftp/cifs have no typed CRD field (global map only; sftp also needs libssh2 in image).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-path
pgbackrest.description: Path where backups/archive are stored inside the repo. Both operators write repoN-path=/pgbackrest/repoN before merging user global, so it is overridable via global. Convention: /pgbackrest/<namespace>/<cluster>/repoN for shared buckets.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-cipher-type
pgbackrest.description: Client-side encryption cipher for the repository. Must be set before stanza creation; cannot be changed on an existing repo.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-cipher-pass
pgbackrest.description: Encryption passphrase; required when repoN-cipher-type=aes-256-cbc. Secure option - do NOT put in the CR global map (rendered into a ConfigMap); supply via configuration Secret projection.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-retention-full-type
pgbackrest.description: Whether repoN-retention-full counts full backups (count) or days (time).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-retention-full
pgbackrest.description: Full backup retention count or days (per retention-full-type). Unset = keep forever (pgBackRest warns). Expire runs automatically after each backup unless expire-auto=n.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-retention-diff
pgbackrest.description: Number of differential backups to retain. Incrementals depending on an expired diff are expired with it.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-retention-archive-type
pgbackrest.description: Backup type WAL retention is applied to.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-retention-archive
pgbackrest.description: Number of backups worth of continuous WAL to retain. DANGEROUS if set below retention-full: can break PITR for older backups.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-retention-history
pgbackrest.description: Days of backup history manifests to retain after expiry (0 = expire immediately).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-bundle
pgbackrest.description: Bundle (combine) small files during backup into larger repo files. Reduces object count on S3/GCS/Azure. Disables repo-hardlink. Cannot be resumed.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-bundle-size
pgbackrest.description: Target size of bundled files (values ~100MiB+ discouraged upstream).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-bundle-limit
pgbackrest.description: Files larger than this are stored individually, not bundled.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-block
pgbackrest.description: Enable block incremental backup (delta at block level inside files). Requires repoN-bundle=y.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-hardlink
pgbackrest.description: Hardlink files between backups (posix repo, bundle off) so each backup looks full.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-symlink
pgbackrest.description: Maintain latest symlink in posix repositories.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-storage-verify-tls
pgbackrest.description: Verify TLS certificate of azure/gcs/s3 object store endpoint. Set n for self-signed MinIO etc. (prefer repoN-storage-ca-file).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-storage-ca-file
pgbackrest.description: Custom CA file used to verify object-store TLS. File must be present in the container: mount via configuration Secret (lands in /etc/pgbackrest/conf.d/).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-storage-ca-path
pgbackrest.description: Directory of CA certificates for object-store TLS verification.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-storage-host
pgbackrest.description: Connect to this host/IP instead of the azure/s3 endpoint (endpoint still used for request signing/virtual-host).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-storage-port
pgbackrest.description: Port for object-store connection.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-storage-tag
pgbackrest.description: Tags applied to objects written to azure/gcs/s3. hash type: one key=value per ini line; the CR global map allows only one value per option name, use configuration Secret for multiple tags.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-storage-upload-chunk-size
pgbackrest.description: Chunk size for multipart/resumable uploads. Larger = fewer requests, more memory per process.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-azure-account
pgbackrest.description: Azure storage account. Secure option - supply via configuration Secret.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-azure-container
pgbackrest.description: Azure blob container for the repository.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-azure-endpoint
pgbackrest.description: Azure blob endpoint.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-azure-key
pgbackrest.description: Azure shared account key or SAS token (per repoN-azure-key-type). Via configuration Secret.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-azure-key-type
pgbackrest.description: Azure auth method; auto = workload-identity/managed identity (pgBackRest >=2.53).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-azure-uri-style
pgbackrest.description: URI style: host = account.endpoint, path = endpoint/account (use path for Azurite/emulators).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-gcs-bucket
pgbackrest.description: GCS bucket for the repository.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-gcs-endpoint
pgbackrest.description: GCS endpoint (change for fake-gcs/proxies).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-gcs-key
pgbackrest.description: Path to GCS service-account JSON key (key-type=service) or token (key-type=token). Put both the conf and the key file in the configuration Secret; files mount under /etc/pgbackrest/conf.d/.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-gcs-key-type
pgbackrest.description: GCS auth method; auto = instance/workload identity metadata.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-gcs-user-project
pgbackrest.description: Project billed for requester-pays buckets.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-bucket
pgbackrest.description: S3 bucket for the repository.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-endpoint
pgbackrest.description: S3 endpoint, e.g. s3.amazonaws.com or MinIO host.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-region
pgbackrest.description: S3 region, e.g. us-east-1.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-key
pgbackrest.description: S3 access key id (key-type=shared). Via configuration Secret (Percona docs: s3.conf in cluster pgbackrest secrets).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-key-secret
pgbackrest.description: S3 secret access key. Via configuration Secret.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-key-type
pgbackrest.description: S3 auth method: shared=static keys, auto=instance profile/IMDS, web-id=IRSA web identity (EKS), pod-id=EKS pod identity, process=external credential process.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-token
pgbackrest.description: S3 session token for temporary credentials.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-kms-key-id
pgbackrest.description: SSE-KMS key id for server-side encryption.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-sse-customer-key
pgbackrest.description: SSE-C customer-provided encryption key (base64).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-role
pgbackrest.description: IAM role for key-type=auto credential lookup (normally discovered automatically).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-requester-pays
pgbackrest.description: Enable requester-pays on S3 requests.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-sts-host
pgbackrest.description: STS host used with key-type=web-id.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-s3-uri-style
pgbackrest.description: host = bucket.endpoint virtual-host style; path = endpoint/bucket (required by most MinIO setups).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-sftp-host
pgbackrest.description: SFTP server host (repo-type=sftp). NOTE: repos[] union has no sftp member in either operator; sftp repos are not really provisionable via CRD and container images may lack libssh2.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-sftp-host-port
pgbackrest.description: SFTP server port.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-sftp-host-user
pgbackrest.description: SFTP username.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-sftp-host-key-check-type
pgbackrest.description: Host key verification mode.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-sftp-host-fingerprint
pgbackrest.description: Expected host fingerprint (check-type=fingerprint/strict).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-sftp-host-key-hash-type
pgbackrest.description: Hash used for host key verification (required for sftp).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-sftp-known-host
pgbackrest.description: Explicit known-hosts entries (check-type accept-new/strict).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-sftp-private-key-file
pgbackrest.description: Path to SSH private key (required for sftp).

pgbackrest.name: repoN-sftp-private-key-passphrase
pgbackrest.description: Passphrase for the private key.

pgbackrest.name: repoN-sftp-public-key-file
pgbackrest.description: Path to SSH public key.

pgbackrest.name: compress-type
pgbackrest.description: Compression for backup files and WAL pushed to the repo. zst recommended when image built with libzstd (both operators' images are).

pgbackrest.name: compress-level
pgbackrest.description: Compression level for compress-type (default and range depend on type).

pgbackrest.name: compress-level-network
pgbackrest.description: lz4 compression applied to uncompressed protocol traffic (pg host <-> repo host).

pgbackrest.name: process-max
pgbackrest.description: Max parallel processes for compress/transfer during backup/restore/archive. Directly trades backup speed vs CPU pressure on the database node - key knob for pgbench interference experiments.

pgbackrest.name: delta
pgbackrest.description: Use file checksums (not timestamps) to detect changed files during backup/restore; restore --delta reuses existing files in PGDATA instead of full copy.

pgbackrest.name: start-fast
pgbackrest.description: Issue an immediate CHECKPOINT so the backup starts without waiting for checkpoint_timeout/checkpoint_completion_target. Causes an IO burst - visible in pgbench latency.

pgbackrest.name: archive-timeout
pgbackrest.description: Seconds to wait for the WAL segment needed to make a backup consistent to reach the archive before failing the backup/check.

pgbackrest.name: archive-async
pgbackrest.description: Asynchronous archive-push/archive-get with parallel workers (process-max) and local spool. Both operators force y in the generated instance config, but a user value in the CR global map is written later and wins.

pgbackrest.name: archive-get-queue-max
pgbackrest.description: Max spool size of WAL prefetched by asynchronous archive-get on replicas.

pgbackrest.name: archive-push-queue-max
pgbackrest.description: Max WAL queue size before archive-push starts DROPPING WAL (reports success to Postgres without storing). Prevents pg_wal filling disk at the cost of broken PITR continuity.

pgbackrest.name: archive-push-batch-size
pgbackrest.description: Total WAL bytes a single async archive-push batch may transfer.

pgbackrest.name: archive-header-check
pgbackrest.description: Check WAL header (version/system-id) matches the stanza on push.

pgbackrest.name: archive-missing-retry
pgbackrest.description: Retry missing WAL segments on archive-get to smooth over archive lag.

pgbackrest.name: spool-path
pgbackrest.description: Local spool directory for async archiving. Operators co-locate it with PGDATA volume; overriding requires the path to exist and be writable.

pgbackrest.name: backup-standby
pgbackrest.description: Perform the backup from a standby (pages copied from standby after primary checkpoint). y fails if no standby; prefer falls back to primary. Requires >=2 replicas and, on Crunchy/Percona, works because all pg hosts are listed in the stanza (pg1..pgN). Key option for offloading backup IO from the primary.

pgbackrest.name: checksum-page
pgbackrest.description: Validate data-page checksums during backup. Operators initdb with --data-checksums, so effectively on.

pgbackrest.name: page-header-check
pgbackrest.description: Validate page headers during backup when page checksums unavailable.

pgbackrest.name: exclude
pgbackrest.description: Paths relative to PGDATA to exclude from the backup (does not apply to restore). Repeatable - use configuration Secret for multiple values.

pgbackrest.name: expire-auto
pgbackrest.description: Run expire automatically after each successful backup.

pgbackrest.name: manifest-save-threshold
pgbackrest.description: How often (bytes backed up) the manifest is checkpointed for resume.

pgbackrest.name: resume
pgbackrest.description: Resume an aborted backup of the same type instead of starting over.

pgbackrest.name: annotation
pgbackrest.description: Key/value annotations stored with the backup (shown by info --set). Crunchy/Percona also auto-annotate manual backups.

pgbackrest.name: archive-check
pgbackrest.description: Verify WAL required for consistency reaches the archive before the backup completes (online only).

pgbackrest.name: archive-copy
pgbackrest.description: Copy required WAL into the backup itself so it is restorable without the archive (requires archive-check).

pgbackrest.name: archive-mode-check
pgbackrest.description: Fail if archive_mode=always is detected (set n to allow, at risk of duplicate archives).

pgbackrest.name: log-level-console
pgbackrest.description: Log level for console/stdout output (what kubectl logs of the job container shows).

pgbackrest.name: log-level-file
pgbackrest.description: Log level for file logging under log-path. Crunchy sets log-level-file=off in job configs when no log volume is available.

pgbackrest.name: log-level-stderr
pgbackrest.description: Log level for stderr output.

pgbackrest.name: log-path
pgbackrest.default: /var/log/pgbackrest (upstream); operator sets a per-pod path on the pgdata/repo volume
pgbackrest.description: Directory for pgBackRest log files. Crunchy 5.8+/Percona expose spec.backups.pgbackrest.log / jobs.log / repoHost.log for path+level tuning.

pgbackrest.name: log-timestamp
pgbackrest.description: Prefix log entries with timestamps.

pgbackrest.name: log-subprocess
pgbackrest.description: Also write logs from local/remote worker subprocesses.

pgbackrest.name: io-timeout
pgbackrest.description: IO timeout for read/write on connections (raise for slow object stores).

pgbackrest.name: protocol-timeout
pgbackrest.description: Max silence on the pgBackRest protocol between processes; must be greater than db-timeout.

pgbackrest.name: db-timeout
pgbackrest.description: Timeout for database queries incl. backup start/stop (pg_backup_start/stop).

pgbackrest.name: buffer-size
pgbackrest.description: IO buffer size (memory use scales with process-max * buffers).

pgbackrest.name: sck-keep-alive
pgbackrest.description: Enable TCP keep-alive on sockets.

pgbackrest.name: tcp-keep-alive-count
pgbackrest.description: Keep-alive probe count before drop.

pgbackrest.name: tcp-keep-alive-idle
pgbackrest.description: Seconds idle before keep-alive probes.

pgbackrest.name: tcp-keep-alive-interval
pgbackrest.description: Seconds between keep-alive probes.

pgbackrest.name: lock-path
pgbackrest.description: Directory for command lock files.

pgbackrest.name: repo-target-time
pgbackrest.description: Read repo state as of a point in time (versioned buckets) for restore/info/repo-* commands (pgBackRest >=2.55).

pgbackrest.name: type (backup)
pgbackrest.description: Backup type. incr auto-promotes to full when no prior full exists.

pgbackrest.name: online
pgbackrest.description: Perform online backup (--no-online only for shut-down clusters; not applicable under operators).

pgbackrest.name: force (backup)
pgbackrest.description: Allow offline backup when cluster is shut down (requires --no-online).

pgbackrest.name: type (restore)
pgbackrest.description: Recovery type: default=recover to end of archive; immediate=stop at consistency; time/lsn/xid/name=PITR to --target; standby=leave as standby; preserve=keep existing recovery settings; none=no recovery config.

pgbackrest.name: target
pgbackrest.description: Recovery target value; required for type=time/lsn/xid/name. Time format e.g. '2026-07-10 15:04:05+00' (must include timezone).

pgbackrest.name: target-action
pgbackrest.description: recovery_target_action once target reached. NOTE for operators: leaving pause keeps the instance in recovery and Patroni will not accept writes until promoted; promote is the usual choice.

pgbackrest.name: target-exclusive
pgbackrest.description: Stop just before the target (exclusive) for type=time/lsn/xid.

pgbackrest.name: target-timeline
pgbackrest.description: Timeline to follow during recovery (PostgreSQL default: latest).

pgbackrest.name: set
pgbackrest.description: Backup set label to restore (e.g. 20260710-000001F) instead of latest; for expire: set to expire (with --repo).

pgbackrest.name: db-include
pgbackrest.description: Restore only listed databases (others become unusable stubs); repeatable.

pgbackrest.name: db-exclude
pgbackrest.description: Exclude databases from restore (with db-include semantics).

pgbackrest.name: link-all
pgbackrest.description: Restore all symlinks recorded in the manifest.

pgbackrest.name: link-map
pgbackrest.description: Remap symlinks on restore.

pgbackrest.name: tablespace-map
pgbackrest.description: Remap tablespaces on restore.

pgbackrest.name: tablespace-map-all
pgbackrest.description: Remap ALL tablespaces under this base path.

pgbackrest.name: recovery-option
pgbackrest.description: Extra recovery GUCs written to postgresql.auto.conf (e.g. recovery_target_inclusive). Only for recovery types that generate recovery settings.

pgbackrest.name: archive-mode (restore)
pgbackrest.description: off disables archiving on the restored host (prevents a cloned cluster from pushing WAL into the source repo).

pgbackrest.name: delta (restore/backup flag)
pgbackrest.description: As --delta on restore: checksum-based in-place restore into non-empty PGDATA (what PGO uses for in-place PITR).

pgbackrest.name: archive-expire-before
pgbackrest.description: Expire WAL older than timestamp regardless of backup retention (advanced; kubectl exec only under operators).

pgbackrest.name: dry-run
pgbackrest.description: Report what expire would remove without removing it.

pgbackrest.name: stanza
pgbackrest.description: Stanza name. Both operators hardcode 'db' (DefaultStanzaName); dataSource restores accept spec.dataSource.pgbackrest.stanza (default db).

patroni.name: ttl
patroni.description: Leader lock TTL - time before automatic failover starts after leader stops updating the lock. Constraint: loop_wait + 2*retry_timeout <= ttl. OPERATOR: value in dynamicConfiguration is OVERWRITTEN by spec.patroni.leaderLeaseDurationSeconds (default 30); set it there. Changing it restarts pods (probe timings derive from it: failureThreshold = ttl/loop_wait).

patroni.name: loop_wait
patroni.description: Seconds the Patroni HA loop sleeps between iterations. Lower = faster reaction, more DCS (Kubernetes API) load. OPERATOR: overwritten by spec.patroni.syncPeriodSeconds (default 10); probes use periodSeconds=syncPeriodSeconds, timeoutSeconds=syncPeriodSeconds/2.

patroni.name: retry_timeout
patroni.description: Timeout for DCS and PostgreSQL operation retries; DCS/network outages shorter than this do not demote the leader. Must satisfy loop_wait + 2*retry_timeout <= ttl.

patroni.name: maximum_lag_on_failover
patroni.description: Max WAL bytes a replica may lag and still participate in leader election. Raise for write-heavy pgbench workloads to avoid failed elections; lower to bound data loss.

patroni.name: maximum_lag_on_syncnode
patroni.description: Max lag before an unhealthy synchronous follower is swapped for a healthy async one; <=0 disables swapping.

patroni.name: max_timelines_history
patroni.description: Max timeline-history items kept in DCS (0 = full history).

patroni.name: primary_start_timeout
patroni.description: Time a failed primary may attempt recovery before failover triggers (0 = fail over immediately on crash detection). Worst-case failover time on primary failure: loop_wait + primary_start_timeout + loop_wait. Legacy alias: master_start_timeout.

patroni.name: primary_stop_timeout
patroni.description: Only with synchronous_mode: seconds allowed for Postgres stop before Patroni SIGKILLs the postmaster; <=0 disables. Legacy alias: master_stop_timeout.

patroni.name: primary_race_backoff
patroni.description: Patroni >=4.1: postpone leader race on standbys while WAL replication from the primary is still advancing, to avoid failovers on briefly unresponsive Patroni.

patroni.name: synchronous_mode
patroni.description: Synchronous replication management: Patroni maintains synchronous_standby_names and only the last leader or a sync replica may win elections. 'quorum' (Patroni >=4.0) uses ANY k(...) quorum commit. Percona/Crunchy: set via dynamicConfiguration; nosync/nofailover instance tags are not exposed by the CRDs.

patroni.name: synchronous_mode_strict
patroni.description: Refuse to disable sync replication when no sync replicas are available - blocks writes (synchronous_standby_names kept/placeholder '__patroni_strict_sync_replica_placeholder__' in Patroni 4.x). Availability trade-off.

patroni.name: synchronous_node_count
patroni.description: Number of synchronous standbys Patroni maintains in synchronous_standby_names (synchronous_mode=on/quorum).

patroni.name: failsafe_mode
patroni.description: Patroni >=3.0 DCS Failsafe Mode: on total DCS (Kubernetes API) outage, primary keeps running if it can reach ALL cluster members via their REST API (POST /failsafe); otherwise demotes. Prevents demotion on API-server blips.

patroni.name: check_timeline
patroni.description: Require candidate's timeline to match the primary's before it may win the leader race.

patroni.name: member_slots_ttl
patroni.allowed: 0 = drop slot as soon as member key expires
patroni.description: Patroni >=4.0 (PG>=11): retention of physical replication slots of members that shut down, protecting WAL for temporarily-down replicas (watch pg_wal growth).

patroni.name: postgresql.use_pg_rewind
patroni.description: Run pg_rewind on a demoted primary so it can rejoin without reinit (needs data checksums or wal_log_hints; operators initdb with --data-checksums). OPERATOR: FORCED to true for postgresVersion > 10 AFTER merging user config - cannot be disabled via CR.

patroni.name: postgresql.use_slots
patroni.description: Use physical replication slots between members. Operator writes use_slots:false as its default but the user's dynamicConfiguration.postgresql section is merged OVER it, so it IS overridable (needed for permanent 'slots' support).

patroni.name: postgresql.remove_data_directory_on_rewind_failure
patroni.description: If pg_rewind fails, wipe PGDATA and reinitialize the replica from scratch (auto-heal at the cost of a full re-clone).

patroni.name: postgresql.remove_data_directory_on_diverged_timelines
patroni.description: If timelines diverged and pg_rewind unusable, wipe PGDATA and re-clone.

patroni.name: postgresql.parameters
patroni.description: PostgreSQL GUCs distributed via DCS. OPERATOR precedence (low->high): operator defaults (e.g. jit=off, password_encryption=scram-sha-256) < dynamicConfiguration.postgresql.parameters < spec.config.parameters < operator-mandatory (unix_socket_directories, ssl* cert paths, wal_level=logical on current main, archive_mode=on, archive_command='pgbackrest ... archive-push', restore_command, log_file_mode; shared_preload_libraries gets mandatory libs prepended). Patroni-controlled GUCs (max_connections, max_wal_senders, max_prepared_transactions, max_locks_per_transaction, wal_level, track_commit_timestamp) change only via DCS and may require restart (pending_restart).

patroni.name: postgresql.pg_hba
patroni.description: pg_hba.conf lines managed by Patroni (ignored if hba_file GUC changed). OPERATOR order: operator-mandatory rules first, then spec.authentication.rules, then dynamicConfiguration.postgresql.pg_hba, then built-in defaults only if no user rules. A bad line can lock out replication - operators prepend their required TLS/replication rules.

patroni.name: postgresql.pg_ident
patroni.description: pg_ident.conf mapping lines (ignored if ident_file GUC set).

patroni.name: standby_cluster.host
patroni.description: Remote primary host for a standby cluster. OPERATOR: managed via spec.standby.{enabled,host,port,repoName}; operator injects host/port and create_replica_methods, deletes user restore_command and sets it from pgBackRest when repoName given.

patroni.name: standby_cluster.port
patroni.description: Remote primary port.

patroni.name: standby_cluster.primary_slot_name
patroni.description: Replication slot on the remote primary to use.

patroni.name: standby_cluster.create_replica_methods
patroni.description: Ordered bootstrap methods for the standby leader. OPERATOR: computed - pgbackrest first when spec.standby.repoName set, basebackup when host set.

patroni.name: standby_cluster.restore_command
patroni.description: WAL fetch command for standby-cluster nodes. OPERATOR: overwritten with the pgBackRest archive-get command when spec.standby.repoName is set.

patroni.name: standby_cluster.archive_cleanup_command
patroni.description: Cleanup command run on the standby leader.

patroni.name: standby_cluster.recovery_min_apply_delay
patroni.description: Delay WAL apply on the standby leader (delayed replica).

patroni.name: slots
patroni.description: Permanent replication slots preserved across switchover/failover; physical slots on all nodes advance every loop_wait (PG>=11); logical slots require use_slots=true and auto-enable hot_standby_feedback. Slot names must be lowercase word chars; slots matching member names persist through member outages.

patroni.name: ignore_slots
patroni.description: Slot matchers Patroni must NOT manage/drop (slots managed outside Patroni). Any matching property subset causes the slot to be ignored.

pgbouncer.name: pool_mode
pgbouncer.description: When a server connection is released back to the pool: session=on client disconnect, transaction=on commit/rollback, statement=after each statement (multi-statement tx forbidden). Also settable per-database/per-user via config.databases/config.users. OPERATOR: safe to set; transaction is the usual choice for pgbench-style high client counts (breaks session features: prepared statements need max_prepared_statements, no SET/LISTEN).

pgbouncer.name: max_client_conn
pgbouncer.description: Max total client connections to PgBouncer (file descriptors must accommodate it).

pgbouncer.name: default_pool_size
pgbouncer.description: Max server connections per user+database pair when no per-db/user pool_size set.

pgbouncer.name: min_pool_size
pgbouncer.description: Keep at least this many server connections in a pool (capped at pool size; only for pools with a forced user or >=1 client). Warms pools after idle periods.

pgbouncer.name: reserve_pool_size
pgbouncer.description: Extra connections allowed to a pool once a client has waited reserve_pool_timeout.

pgbouncer.name: reserve_pool_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Client wait time before the reserve pool is used; 0 disables.

pgbouncer.name: max_db_connections
pgbouncer.description: Server-connection cap per database (all users combined). Also per-db in config.databases.

pgbouncer.name: max_db_client_connections
pgbouncer.description: PgBouncer >=1.23: client-connection cap per database.

pgbouncer.name: max_user_connections
pgbouncer.description: Server-connection cap per user (all databases). Also per-user in config.users.

pgbouncer.name: max_user_client_connections
pgbouncer.description: PgBouncer >=1.23: client-connection cap per user.

pgbouncer.name: server_round_robin
pgbouncer.description: Use server connections round-robin (LIFO default concentrates load on few connections).

pgbouncer.name: server_idle_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Close server connections idle longer than this; 0 disables.

pgbouncer.name: server_lifetime
pgbouncer.description: Close unused server connections older than this; 0 = use once then close. Also per-db.

pgbouncer.name: server_connect_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Abandon connect+login to PostgreSQL after this long.

pgbouncer.name: server_login_retry
pgbouncer.description: Wait after failed server login before retrying (new clients error immediately during the wait).

pgbouncer.name: server_reset_query
pgbouncer.description: Query run when a server connection is released (session pooling). Lighter alternative: DEALLOCATE ALL.

pgbouncer.name: server_reset_query_always
pgbouncer.description: Run server_reset_query in all pool modes (workaround for session state under transaction pooling).

pgbouncer.name: server_check_delay
pgbouncer.description: Reuse a released connection without health check if it was checked within this window; 0 = always check.

pgbouncer.name: server_check_query
pgbouncer.description: Sanity-check query for idle server connections; empty string disables checking.

pgbouncer.name: server_fast_close
pgbouncer.description: In session pooling, close 'close_needed' server connections at transaction end rather than session end (faster config/DNS rollover, may interrupt idle sessions).

pgbouncer.name: query_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Cancel queries running longer than this (client also disconnected). Keep slightly larger than server-side statement_timeout - last-resort network guard.

pgbouncer.name: query_wait_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Max time a query may wait in queue for a server connection before the client is disconnected. Primary knob behind 'query_wait_timeout' errors during saturation/failover.

pgbouncer.name: cancel_wait_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Max time a cancel request may wait for forwarding before its client is dropped.

pgbouncer.name: client_idle_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Disconnect clients idle longer than this (dangerous with app-side pooled idle connections).

pgbouncer.name: client_login_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Disconnect clients that fail to complete login within this time.

pgbouncer.name: idle_transaction_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Disconnect clients 'idle in transaction' longer than this.

pgbouncer.name: transaction_timeout
pgbouncer.description: PgBouncer >=1.24: disconnect clients in a transaction longer than this.

pgbouncer.name: autodb_idle_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Free unused wildcard ('*') database pools after this idle time (stats forgotten). Relevant: both operators define databases via '*' by default.

pgbouncer.name: pool_idle_timeout
pgbouncer.description: PgBouncer >=1.25: free individual idle pools (db/user pair) after this many seconds.

pgbouncer.name: suspend_timeout
pgbouncer.description: Buffer-flush wait during SUSPEND/online restart before dropping connections.

pgbouncer.name: ignore_startup_parameters
pgbouncer.description: Startup parameters PgBouncer should accept-and-ignore rather than error on (e.g. options, extra_float_digits). OPERATOR: operator default: 'extra_float_digits' (for JDBC). Overriding REPLACES it - include extra_float_digits in any custom value.

pgbouncer.name: track_extra_parameters
pgbouncer.description: Extra GUCs tracked per-client and restored on server reuse (only parameters PostgreSQL reports to clients). client_encoding/datestyle/timezone/standard_conforming_strings/application_name are always tracked.

pgbouncer.name: tcp_keepalive
pgbouncer.description: Enable TCP keepalive with OS defaults on sockets.

pgbouncer.name: tcp_keepcnt
pgbouncer.description: Keepalive probe count.

pgbouncer.name: tcp_keepidle
pgbouncer.description: Idle time before keepalive probes.

pgbouncer.name: tcp_keepintvl
pgbouncer.description: Interval between keepalive probes.

pgbouncer.name: tcp_user_timeout
pgbouncer.description: TCP_USER_TIMEOUT: max unacked transmit time before the kernel closes the connection (Linux only).

pgbouncer.name: tcp_defer_accept
pgbouncer.description: TCP_DEFER_ACCEPT socket option (value hardcoded 45s when enabled).

pgbouncer.name: tcp_socket_buffer
pgbouncer.description: Socket buffer size override.

pgbouncer.name: listen_backlog
pgbouncer.description: listen(2) backlog for new connection attempts (startup-only, needs restart).

pgbouncer.name: so_reuseport
pgbouncer.description: SO_REUSEPORT for running multiple pgbouncer processes on one port (not applicable to operator single-process pods; scale spec.proxy.pgBouncer.replicas instead).

pgbouncer.name: pkt_buf
pgbouncer.description: Internal packet buffer size.

pgbouncer.name: max_packet_size
pgbouncer.description: Max PostgreSQL packet size allowed through.

pgbouncer.name: sbuf_loopcnt
pgbouncer.description: Data-processing loops per connection event before yielding (0=no limit).

pgbouncer.name: max_prepared_statements
pgbouncer.description: Track/replay protocol-level named prepared statements across server connections in transaction/statement pooling. Essential for pgbench -M prepared through transaction pooling.

pgbouncer.name: scram_iterations
pgbouncer.description: Iterations when encrypting passwords with SCRAM-SHA-256.

pgbouncer.name: auth_type
pgbouncer.description: Client authentication method. md5 auto-upgrades to SCRAM when the stored secret is SCRAM. OPERATOR: neither operator sets auth_type (effective default md5). The operator wires auth_file+auth_user+auth_query (Crunchy/Percona: SELECT username,password FROM pgbouncer.get_auth($1) as _crunchypgbouncer against the primary). Changing auth_type to hba/cert/ldap requires supplying files via config.files and is unsupported territory; scram-sha-256 works only because verifiers come from auth_query - frontend SCRAM cannot do channel binding through PgBouncer.

pgbouncer.name: auth_file
pgbouncer.description: userlist file with credentials. OPERATOR: operator-managed Secret projection containing the _crunchypgbouncer credential; override only with config.files.

pgbouncer.name: auth_user
pgbouncer.description: User for auth_query lookups of users missing from auth_file. OPERATOR: operator-set; overridable via global but breaks managed auth.

pgbouncer.name: auth_query
pgbouncer.default: SELECT rolname, CASE WHEN rolvaliduntil < now() THEN NULL ELSE rolpassword END FROM pg_authid WHERE rolname=$1 AND rolcanlogin (upstream)
pgbouncer.description: Query to fetch password verifier for a user. OPERATOR: operator sets SELECT username, password from pgbouncer.get_auth($1) (function installed in each database by the operator).

pgbouncer.name: auth_dbname
pgbouncer.description: Database used for auth_query when the target db is inaccessible.

pgbouncer.name: admin_users
pgbouncer.description: Users allowed to connect to the pgbouncer admin console with full rights.

pgbouncer.name: stats_users
pgbouncer.description: Users allowed read-only console access (SHOW STATS etc.). OPERATOR: Crunchy sets stats_users=_crunchypgbouncer when OpenTelemetry metrics enabled; Percona PMM sidecar likewise queries SHOW via the operator user.

pgbouncer.name: stats_period
pgbouncer.description: Aggregation period for SHOW STATS averages and periodic log_stats lines.

pgbouncer.name: log_connections
pgbouncer.description: Log successful client logins.

pgbouncer.name: log_disconnections
pgbouncer.description: Log client disconnects with reason.

pgbouncer.name: log_pooler_errors
pgbouncer.description: Log pooler errors sent to clients.

pgbouncer.name: log_stats
pgbouncer.description: Write aggregated stats to log every stats_period.

pgbouncer.name: verbose
pgbouncer.description: Log verbosity (1=some debug, 2=noisy). Increase temporarily when debugging pool behavior.

pgbouncer.name: application_name_add_host
pgbouncer.description: Append client host:port to application_name at connection start.

pgbouncer.name: disable_pqexec
pgbouncer.description: Reject Simple Query protocol (multi-statement packets) for security.

pgbouncer.name: client_tls_sslmode
pgbouncer.description: TLS mode for client->PgBouncer connections. OPERATOR: operator default: require, with operator-issued frontend cert (client_tls_cert_file/key/ca preset). Overridable via global; setting disable turns off TLS to PgBouncer.

pgbouncer.name: server_tls_sslmode
pgbouncer.description: TLS mode for PgBouncer->PostgreSQL connections. OPERATOR: operator default: verify-full with cluster CA (server_tls_ca_file preset).

pgbouncer.name: listen_addr
pgbouncer.description: Addresses to listen on. OPERATOR: operator forces '*'; do not override (breaks Service traffic). Startup-only option.

pgbouncer.name: listen_port
pgbouncer.description: Port PgBouncer listens on. OPERATOR: operator sets from spec.proxy.pgBouncer.port (default 5432, CRD min 1024). Do not set via global - change the CRD field so Service/probes stay in sync.

pgbouncer.name: unix_socket_dir
pgbouncer.description: Unix socket directory. OPERATOR: operator sets empty (sockets disabled, read-only fs). Leave alone.

pgbouncer.name: conffile
pgbouncer.description: Location of the main config file. OPERATOR: LOCKED - operator writes conffile after merging user settings; cannot be overridden via config.global.

pgbouncer.name: logfile
pgbouncer.description: Additional log file destination (stdout logging always on in containers). OPERATOR: Crunchy CRD validation (current main): value must end with '.log'; auto-set when OpenTelemetry logs enabled. config.global map is limited to 50 keys (CRD MaxProperties).
